rw [wignerMatrixMeasure]
  -- Use the fact that the WignerMeasure is a probability measure and the wignerMatrixMap is measurable.
  apply Measure.map_isProbabilityMeasure _ _ 
  -- Prove that WignerMeasure is a probability measure.
  <;> simp [IsProbabilityMeasure]
  <;>
    (try
      exact (by
        -- For n = 0, the space is trivial and WignerMeasure is a point measure.
        simp_all [WignerMeasure, pi_isProbabilityMeasure]
        <;>
          aesop
        )
    )
  <;>
    (try
      exact
        (by
          -- For n > 0, WignerMeasure is the product of probability measures and hence is a probability measure.
          simp_all [WignerMeasure, pi_isProbabilityMeasure]
          <;>
            aesop
        )
    )
  <;>
    (try
      exact
        (by
          -- Use the fact that the product of probability measures is a probability measure.
          simp_all [WignerMeasure, pi_isProbabilityMeasure]
          <;>
            aesop
        )
    )

rw [wignerMatrixMeasure]
  apply Measure.map.isProbabilityMeasure
  <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [WignerMeasure]) <;>
    (try assumption) <;>
    (try infer_instance)
  <;>
    (try
      {
        have h₁ : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          infer_instance
        exact h₁.isProbabilityMeasure
      })
  <;>
    (try
      {
        have h₂ : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
          infer_instance
        exact h₂.isProbabilityMeasure
      })
  <;>
    (try aesop)
EXCEPTION: TimeoutError:

-- Use the fact that the pushforward of a probability measure under a measurable function is a probability measure.
  have h : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- The product of probability measures is a probability measure.
    infer_instance
  -- The function `wignerMatrixMap n` is measurable (by `wignerMatrixMapMeasurable n`).
  have h₂ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    apply wignerMatrixMapMeasurable
  -- The pushforward of a probability measure under a measurable function is a probability measure.
  exact h.map h₂
